prompt: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?
choices: They are two names for the same check
Authentication proves who you are; authorization decides what you may do
Authentication decides what you may do; authorization proves who you are
Authentication runs on the server; authorization always runs in the browser

prompt: A logged-in user changes an ID in an API request and receives another customer's record. Which two principles were violated?
choices: Row-level access control was not enforced
The client was trusted instead of the server enforcing ownership
Passwords were stored in plain text
The database used too many tables

prompt: Which statement best describes the principle of least privilege?
choices: Give every user admin rights so nothing is blocked
Grant each user and component only the minimum access it needs
Hide sensitive buttons in the user interface
Require a password that is at least eight characters long

prompt: Why must authorization checks be enforced on the server rather than only in the browser?
choices: Anything running on the client can be inspected and modified by the user
